title: populus deltoides var. virginiana
intro: Populus deltoides var. virginiana, also known as eastern cottonwood, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The bark and leaves of the eastern cottonwood are rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, providing pain relief, reducing inflammation, and alleviating fever. The plant is prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, offering various health benefits, including cardiovascular support and digestive relief.
